title: More Is Expected of You Now
prose: Seventh-years end up, whether they asked for it or not, somewhere near the top of the school's unofficial pecking order — younger students ask you for directions like you're supposed to know everything, and more than a few professors have started treating classroom discussions as something closer to a conversation between colleagues.

It's a strange kind of authority, mostly earned just by having survived this long, but there's real weight to it too — a first-year genuinely relieved to have a seventh-year vouch for them, a prefect's badge on someone's chest carrying more consequence than it used to.

choices.label: It sits a little uneasily with you, honestly.
